title: polypodium lingulatum
intro: Polypodium lingulatum, also known as the Lemon Maidens Fern or New Zealand Polypody, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Polypodium lingulatum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
